response:
*KEEP THE AREA CLEAN*  
BRUSH GENTLY AND THOROUGHLY TWO TIMES A DAY  
RINSE WITH WATER AFTER EVERY MEAL  

*FLOSS CAREFULLY*  
FLOSS AROUND THE AFFECTED TOOTH  
REMOVE FOOD PARTICLES AND PREVENT DAMAGE  

*AVOID CERTAIN FOODS*  
STAY AWAY FROM HARD, STICKY, OR CHERRY FOODS  
EAT MODERATELY AND MONITOR FOR SIGNS OF INFECTION  

*USE TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS*  
TEMPORARY FILLING MATERIALS ARE AVAILABLE IN PHARMACIES  
USE THEM CAREFULLY AND FOLLOW INSTRUCTIONS  

*PAIN MANAGEMENT*  
USE OVER-the-counter PAIN RELIEVERS LIKE IBUPROFEN  
AVOID ASPIRIN DIRECTLY ON THE GUMS OR CAVITY  

*SAINT WATER RINSE*  
RINSE WITH SALT WATER TO REDUCE INFLAMMATION  
MIX 1/2 TEASPOON SALT IN 8 OUNCES OF WARM WATER  

*AVOID EXTREME TEMPERATURES*  
CHEW ON THE OPPOSITE SIDE OF THE MOUTH  
TO PREVENT PRESSURE ON THE DAMAGED TOOTH  

*MONITOR FOR CHANGES*  
KEEP AN EYE ON THE TOOTH FOR SIGNS OF INFECTION  
INCREASE PAIN OR SENSITIVITY MAY INDICATE A PROBLEM  

*SEE DENTAL CARE IMMEDIATELY*  
DENTAL CARE IS CRITICAL EVEN IN ABROAD LOCATIONS  
DENTAL SCHOOLS OR EXPATIATE COMMUNITIES CAN PROVIDE HELP  

*DIAGNOSTIC EMERGENCIES*  
SEVERE PAIN OR SWELLING MAY INDICATE AN ABSCESS  
SEE A DENTIST IMMEDIATELY TO PREVENT COMPLICATIONS
